title: Odisha sizzles under severe heatwave condition; Hirakud boils at 44 deg C, Bhubaneswar at 39.6 - Odisha TV
full_text:
Odisha continues to bake under severe heatwave, with temperatures soaring to unprecedented levels. In Hirakud, the mercury touched a staggering 44 degrees Celsius by mid-afternoon on Tuesday, making it one of the hottest spots in the state. Jharsuguda closely followed, recording 43.8 degrees, while Sambalpur also crossed the 43-degree mark.
The extreme heat has disrupted daily life, forcing residents to seek shade, hydrate constantly, and adjust their routines to cope with the sweltering conditions. Streets that were usually bustling have slowed down, as people try to avoid the harsh midday sun.
Bhubaneswar and Coastal Regions Feel the Burn
Odisha's capital, Bhubaneswar, is not far behind, with the day’s maximum temperature reaching 39.6 degrees celsius. Coastal towns and interior regions are also experiencing high levels of discomfort as the relentless sun continues its assault.
Citizens report feeling drained and fatigued, with outdoor work and travel becoming increasingly challenging. Health experts have urged the public to take necessary precautions, stay indoors during peak hours, and maintain adequate hydration.
With the heatwave showing no immediate signs of easing, communities across Odisha are bracing themselves for several more days of intense temperatures.
This record-breaking heat serves as a stark reminder of the extreme weather events that can disrupt daily life, highlighting the importance of awareness and preparedness during such climatic challenges.
